docs.text: Check Item Description Failure Reason Xposed Framework Active Detect Xposed framework running status Framework not installed or not activated Module Loaded Confirm NoWakeLock is recognized by framework Module not checked or installation failed Module Activated Verify module is running in target process Scope configuration error

docs.title: Function Checks
docs.text:
Check Item Description Failure Reason Hook Working Normally Verify system call interception functionality System version incompatible Configuration Read Success Confirm settings can be read Permission issues or storage anomaly Database Normal Check database read/write functionality Insufficient storage space or permission issues

[Screenshot needed: Module check page - all successful]

docs.title: ✅ Normal Status
docs.text: All check items show green icons, indicating the module is working normally.

docs.title: ❌ Abnormal Status
docs.text: Red icons indicate problems that need to be addressed.

docs.title: ⚠️ Warning Status
docs.text: Yellow icons indicate partially limited functionality but basic usability.

docs.title: Xposed Framework Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Xposed Framework Active" shows ❌

Solution Steps: 1. Confirm LSPosed or EdXposed is installed 2. Check if framework manager shows "Activated" 3. Restart device 4. Verify framework version compatibility

docs.title: Module Loading Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Module Loaded" shows ❌

Solution Steps: 1. Open Xposed Manager 2. Go to "Modules" page 3. Confirm NoWakeLock is checked 4. Restart device 5. Recheck

docs.title: Scope Configuration Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Module Activated" shows ❌

Solution Steps: 1. Open LSPosed Manager 2. Click NoWakeLock module 3. Enter "Scope" settings 4. Confirm the following are selected: - android (System Framework) - com.js.nowakelock (Application itself) 5. Restart device

[Screenshot needed: LSPosed scope configuration]

docs.title: Hook Function Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Hook Working Normally" shows ❌

Possible Causes: - Unsupported Android version - System customization causing interface changes - SELinux policy restrictions

Solutions: 1. Check device compatibility 2. View Xposed logs: 

adb logcat | grep -i nowakelock
3. Try reinstalling module

docs.title: Configuration Reading Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Configuration Read Success" shows ❌

Solution Steps: 1. Check storage permissions 2. Clear application data: 

# Note: This will delete all configurations
adb shell pm clear com.js.nowakelock
3. Reopen application

docs.title: Database Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Database Normal" shows ❌

Solution Steps: 1. Check storage space 2. Verify application permissions 3. Reset database: - Settings → Clear Data - Reconfigure

docs.title: View System Logs
docs.text:
# View NoWakeLock related logs
adb logcat | grep -i nowakelock

# View Xposed logs
adb logcat | grep -i xposed

docs.title: Verify Hook Effects
docs.text:
1. Open any application
  2. Switch to WakeLock page
  3. Check if new WakeLock records appear

docs.title: Test Rule Functionality
docs.text:
1. Set a test rule
  2. Trigger corresponding system behavior
  3. Check if statistics data updates

docs.title: Resource Usage
docs.text: The module check page also displays: - CPU usage rate - Memory usage - Storage space usage

docs.title: Performance Metrics
docs.text: Metric Normal Range Description CPU Usage < 5% Hook processing overhead Memory Usage < 50MB Cache and data usage Storage Usage < 100MB Database and log size

docs.title: Recommended Frequency
docs.text:
• First Installation: Check daily
  • Stable Operation: Check weekly
  • After System Updates: Check immediately

docs.title: Automatic Checks
docs.text: The application automatically performs basic checks on startup and shows notifications when anomalies occur.

docs.title: Check Records
docs.text: The application keeps check records for the last 30 days, viewable in settings.

docs.title: Contact Support
docs.text:
If checks continue to fail:

  1. Collect Information:
  2. Device model and Android version
  3. Xposed framework type and version

  4. Module check page screenshot

  5. Get Logs: 

    adb logcat -v time > nowakelock_log.txt

  6. Seek Help:

  7. GitHub Issues
  8. Telegram Group
  9. Discord Community

Important Reminder

When module check fails, NoWakeLock functionality may not work properly. Please resolve all issues before proceeding with configuration.

docs.title: Problem Analysis Guide
docs.text:
Before configuring any rules, you must first confirm that your device actually has battery issues. This guide explains how to use tools to analyze and identify problems.

Important Principle

Only after confirming through data analysis that a specific WakeLock/Alarm/Service is actually causing standby battery drain should you consider implementing restrictions. Do not configure based on assumptions or preconceptions.

docs.title: Recommended Analysis Tools
docs.text:
1. BetterBatteryStats (BBS) - First choice, provides most comprehensive analysis
  2. NoWakeLock Built-in Statistics - Alternative when BBS is unavailable
  3. Combined Use - For more accurate diagnostic results

docs.title: Diagnostic Steps
docs.text:
1. Install BBS and grant necessary permissions
  2. Set monitoring period - Recommend monitoring complete standby cycles
  3. Analyze key data:
  4. Kernel Wakelocks (system-level wake locks)
  5. Partial Wakelocks (application-level wake locks)
  6. Wake frequency and duration for each app

docs.title: Problem Identification Criteria
docs.text:
• Review abnormal wake activity during device standby periods
  • Identify WakeLocks with excessive duration or frequency
  • Confirm these activities actually cause battery consumption issues

docs.title: Using NoWakeLock Built-in Data Analysis
docs.text: When BBS is unavailable, you can use NoWakeLock's own statistical data:

docs.title: Data Viewing Methods
docs.text:
1. WakeLock List - View WakeLock activity statistics for each app
  2. Sort by Duration - Identify abnormally long-duration WakeLocks
  3. Sort by Frequency - Discover abnormally high-frequency WakeLocks
  4. Active Status - Monitor currently held WakeLocks

docs.title: Abnormality Identification Criteria
docs.text:
• WakeLocks showing active during device standby periods
  • Single WakeLock cumulative duration far exceeding other similar apps
  • Frequent WakeLock acquisition when app is not in use
  • Abnormal WakeLock activity during nighttime hours

docs.title: Step 1: Confirm Problem Exists
docs.text:
1. Use analysis tools to monitor a complete usage cycle (at least 24 hours)
  2. Prioritize using BBS for comprehensive monitoring
  3. Use NoWakeLock built-in statistics when BBS is unavailable
  4. Compare standby period WakeLock activity with battery consumption
  5. Confirm abnormalities - Look for WakeLocks active when device is not in use

docs.title: Step 2: Precisely Locate Problem Source
docs.text:
1. Identify specific apps and services causing abnormal WakeLocks
  2. Analyze patterns - Check if issues occur during specific time periods or conditions
  3. Assess impact - Confirm actual impact level of the WakeLock on battery life

docs.title: Step 3: Minimize Intervention
docs.text:
1. Prioritize app settings - Check if there are relevant options within the app
  2. Test restriction effects - Use limit mode rather than complete blocking
  3. Monitor results - Continue monitoring effects after configuration

Diagnostic Key Points

  • Problems must actually exist, not be theoretical
  • Intervention must be targeted, not preventive
  • Verify app functionality remains normal after each configuration

docs.title: Post-Configuration Monitoring
docs.text:
Necessary Steps: 1. Continue data monitoring effects after configuration - Prioritize using BBS for comprehensive evaluation - Also use NoWakeLock built-in statistics as reference 2. Verify app functionality - Confirm all app functions still work normally 3. Evaluate battery improvement - Compare actual battery consumption before and after configuration

Rollback Preparation: - If app functionality is affected, immediately cancel configuration - If battery improvement is not significant, re-evaluate necessity of restrictions

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
After problem analysis completion:

  1. WakeLock Management - Configure specific WakeLock rules
  2. Quick Start - 5-minute quick configuration guide
  3. FAQ - Solutions when encountering problems

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: 5-minute quick configuration guide to start optimizing your device's battery life with NoWakeLock.

docs.title: Step 1: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Open NoWakeLock and go to the "Module Check" page:

  • ✅ All items show green = Installation successful
  • ❌ Any red items = Check the Installation Guide

  2. Browse the installed applications list
  3. Note the statistics on the right side

List Information: - Application name and icon - WakeLock/Alarm/Service statistics - Last activity time

docs.title: Step 3: Analyze Battery Issues
docs.text: ⚠️ Important: Before configuring any rules, you must first confirm that your device actually has battery issues.

docs.title: Use Problem Analysis Tools
docs.text:

  • Use BetterBatteryStats or NoWakeLock built-in statistics to confirm issues

docs.title: Quick Problem Application Identification

docs.title: Configure Problem WakeLocks
docs.text:
1. Click on abnormal WakeLock items
  2. Select handling method:
  3. Limit - Timeout release (recommended for beginners)
  4. Block - Completely prevent (use with caution)

docs.title: Check After 24 Hours
docs.text:
1. Apps interface - View blocking effects

docs.title: Key Indicators

  • App notifications work normally ✅

docs.title: App Cannot Receive Notifications
docs.text: Solution: 1. Find the app's push-related WakeLocks 2. Change to "Allow" mode 3. Observe for several hours

docs.title: No Effect After Setting
docs.text: Check Items: 1. Is module working normally 2. Are rules applied correctly 3. Has app restarted

docs.title: Device Becomes Slow or Laggy

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Collection of the most commonly encountered questions and solutions.

docs.title: Q: Module doesn't work after installation?
docs.text: A: Check the following items: 1. Confirm the Xposed framework is running normally 2. Check the NoWakeLock module in the framework manager 3. Restart the device and check module status 4. Confirm scope includes android system framework

docs.title: Q: Which Xposed frameworks are supported?
docs.text: A: Supported frameworks: - LSPosed - Recommended, best compatibility - EdXposed - Supported on some devices - TaiChi - Not recommended, may have compatibility issues

docs.title: Q: Module check shows red ❌?
docs.text: A: Handle based on specific item: - Framework not activated → Check Xposed installation - Module not loaded → Confirm module is checked and restart - Hook failed → May be system version incompatibility

docs.title: Q: Rules have no effect after setting?
docs.text: A: Possible reasons: 1. Rules not effective - Check if rules are enabled 2. App restart - Some rules require app restart 3. System cache - Wait a few minutes for system to apply new rules 4. Permission issues - Confirm NoWakeLock has necessary permissions

docs.title: Q: Some apps cannot receive push notifications?
docs.text: A: Solution steps: 1. View the app's WakeLock list 2. Find push-related WakeLocks (usually contain Push, GCM, FCM) 3. Change these WakeLocks to "Allow" mode 4. Restart app to test push functionality

docs.title: Q: Does app become laggy or function abnormally?
docs.text: A: Immediate actions: 1. Temporarily disable all rules for that app 2. Enable rules one by one to find the problem rule 3. Adjust problem rule parameters or change to limit mode 4. Avoid intercepting critical system services

docs.title: Q: How to determine if optimization is effective?
docs.text: A: Observe metrics: - Short term (24 hours): Check interception statistics, app functions normally - Medium term (one week): Battery life significantly improved - Long term: Overall system smoothness improved

docs.title: Q: Android version compatibility?
docs.text: A: Support status: - Android 7.0-11 - Full support - Android 12-13 - Basic support, some features may be limited - Android 14-15 - Experimental support, may have issues

docs.title: Q: Specific brand device issues?
docs.text: A: Known issues: - MIUI - Need to disable MIUI optimization, allow background running - ColorOS - May need to allow auto-start in permission management - EMUI - Recommend adding NoWakeLock to protected apps - OneUI - ⚠️ Currently not supported - Due to OneUI modifying Android source code, Hook locations have been tried with various methods but consistently fail to work

docs.title: Q: Module fails after system update?
docs.text: A: Handling steps: 1. Check if Xposed framework still works 2. Confirm NoWakeLock module is still activated 3. Reinstall module if necessary 4. Reconfigure scope settings

docs.title: Q: NoWakeLock itself consumes battery?
docs.text:
A: Under normal circumstances NoWakeLock consumes very little battery: - CPU usage < 1% - Memory usage < 50MB - Background activity minimized

If abnormal battery consumption is found, please check configuration or contact support.

docs.title: Q: Device becomes slow or laggy?
docs.text: A: Possible reasons: 1. Too many rules - Reduce unnecessary rules 2. Complex regex - Simplify regular expressions 3. Frequent interception - Adjust interception strategy 4. Insufficient memory - Restart device to free memory

docs.title: Q: App startup becomes slow?
docs.text: A: Check if: 1. Intercepted Services necessary for app startup 2. Over-restricted initialization-related WakeLocks 3. Rules affected app startup process

docs.title: Q: Statistics data inaccurate?
docs.text: A: Possible situations: 1. Data delay - Statistics update every 5 minutes 2. Device restart - Some real-time data reset after restart 3. Timezone issues - Check device timezone settings 4. Storage issues - Clear database cache

docs.title: Q: Historical data lost?
docs.text: A: Data retention policy: - Real-time data - Cleared after device restart - Statistics data - Retained for 30 days - Configuration data - Permanently saved (unless manually cleared)

docs.title: Q: Exported configuration cannot be imported?
docs.text: A: Check items: 1. File format correct (JSON) 2. Version compatibility 3. File not corrupted 4. Sufficient permissions

docs.title: Q: How to use in multi-user environment?
docs.text: A: Notes: 1. Each user needs separate configuration 2. System apps share settings across all users 3. Configurations don't affect each other when switching users 4. Some features require primary user permissions

docs.title: Q: Cannot use in work profile?
docs.text: A: Work profile limitations: 1. Need to install Xposed framework separately in work profile 2. Some enterprise policies may prohibit Xposed modules 3. Contact administrator to confirm related policies

docs.title: Q: Is NoWakeLock safe?
docs.text: A: Security measures: - Open source code - All code publicly available for review - No network permissions - Won't upload any data - Local storage - All data saved only on device locally - Minimal permissions - Only requests necessary system permissions

docs.title: Q: Will it affect system stability?
docs.text: A: Safety considerations: 1. Use system standard Hook mechanism 2. Minimize impact on system calls 3. Automatic degradation handling in exception cases 4. Don't modify system core files

docs.title: Q: Will privacy data leak?
docs.text: A: Privacy protection: - No data collection - Don't collect any personal information - No network communication - Don't communicate with external servers - Local processing - All analysis performed locally on device - User control - Users have complete control over all data

docs.title: Q: What to do if completely unable to use?
docs.text: A: Reset steps: 1. Disable module in Xposed 2. Restart device 3. Clear NoWakeLock app data 4. Re-enable module and configure

docs.title: Q: Some apps frequently crash?
docs.text: A: Emergency handling: 1. Immediately disable all rules for that app 2. Check error information in system logs 3. Gradually restore rules and observe 4. Add app to whitelist if necessary

docs.title: Q: How to collect debug information?
docs.text:
A: Information collection: 

# Device information
adb shell getprop ro.build.version.release
adb shell getprop ro.product.model

# App logs
adb logcat | grep -i nowakelock

# Xposed logs
adb logcat | grep -i xposed

docs.title: Q: What are WakeLock/Alarm/Service? How to configure for best results?
docs.text:
A: Core concept explanation:

WakeLock (Wake Lock): - Mechanism to prevent device from entering sleep state - Types: PARTIAL (CPU running), SCREEN (screen on), etc. - Official documentation: Android WakeLock Guide

Alarm (Scheduled Tasks): - System timer that triggers tasks at specific times - Types: RTC, ELAPSED_REALTIME, etc. - Official documentation: Android Alarms Guide

Service (Services): - App components running in background - Types: foreground service, background service, bound service - Official documentation: Android Services Guide

Recommended learning resources: - XDA Guide: "Complete WakeLock Guide for Beginners" - Amplify - Provides WakeLock/Alarm/Service information reference lists - WakeBlock - Also provides WakeLock information

Note: Unfortunately there's no perfect universal reference, differences between devices are huge. Need to adjust based on specific device and apps.

docs.title: Q: What to do if device won't boot due to incorrect operations?
docs.text:
A: Emergency recovery steps:

Situation 1: Boot issues caused by NoWakeLock module:

Method 1: Hardware button safe mode (Recommended): 

1. Long press power button for 10 seconds to force restart
2. Immediately after screen goes black, repeatedly press any hardware button (volume or power)
3. After feeling 2 short vibrations, continue quickly pressing same button 4 times
4. After 4th press feel long vibration, indicating Xposed is disabled
5. After normal boot, disable NoWakeLock module in LSPosed

Method 2: Recovery filesystem method: 

1. Enter TWRP Recovery
2. Tap Advanced → File Manager
3. Navigate to /data/adb/lspd/
4. Delete config folder
5. Restart to system

Method 3: Device-specific method (e.g., Pixel): 

Press volume down frantically after Google Logo appears during boot
Until device vibrates confirming safe mode entry

Situation 2: Mistakenly intercepted important system components: 

1. Enter Recovery → File Manager
2. Navigate to /data/misc/xxx-xxx-xxx/prefs/com.js.nowakelock
   # xxx-xxx-xxx is a long random string, may be different for each device
3. Delete entire folder
4. Restart device
5. After entering system restore misoperation, if unclear about specific problem, directly clear NoWakeLock data

When cause is uncertain: - After entering system directly clear NoWakeLock app data - Reconfigure, avoid intercepting critical system components

Important Warning

Always backup configuration before modifying critical system components. Recommend starting debugging from third-party apps, gradually adjusting system services.

docs.title: Q: Does NoWakeLock collect privacy data?
docs.text:
A: Privacy protection commitment: - Completely local - All data stored only locally on device - Zero data upload - No data uploaded to any servers - No privacy collection - Don't collect or store any personal information - Open source transparency - Source code completely public and reviewable

Possible future features: - May add optional cloud configuration sync feature - Users have complete control over whether to enable - Will still adhere to privacy protection principles

docs.title: Q: What to do if need new features or found bugs?
docs.text: A: Participation methods: - GitHub Issues - Submit issues or feature requests - Detailed description - Provide complete problem description and reproduction steps - Active feedback - Developers will try their best to handle all feedback

docs.title: Q: How to help update translations?
docs.text: A: Translation contributions: - Pull Request - Submit translation PR directly - Multi-language support - Welcome translation contributions in various languages - Community collaboration - Can discuss translation details in community groups

docs.title: Q: Where to report issues?
docs.text: A: Support channels: - GitHub Issues - Detailed technical issues and feature requests - Telegram Group - @nowakelock Quick questions and discussions - Discord Community - NoWakelock In-depth technical exchanges

docs.title: Q: How to provide effective issue reports?
docs.text: A: Include information: 1. Device info - Brand, model, Android version 2. Framework info - Xposed framework type and version 3. Problem description - Specific problem phenomena 4. Reproduction steps - How to reproduce the problem 5. Log information - Relevant error logs 6. Screenshots - Screenshots of problem interface

docs.title: Q: How long to get response?
docs.text:
A: Response time: - Community groups - Usually within a few hours - GitHub Issues - 1-3 business days - Emergency issues - Will be prioritized

Usage Suggestions

When encountering problems, recommend checking documentation and FAQ first. If problem persists, please provide detailed information to better assist in resolving.

Important Reminder

Please backup important data before use. Incorrect configuration may affect device normal operation, please operate carefully.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Technical terms and concepts related to NoWakeLock.

docs.title: WakeLock (Wake Lock)
docs.text:
Mechanism to prevent Android devices from entering sleep state. Apps hold WakeLocks to keep CPU running or screen on.

Types: - PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK - Keep CPU running, screen can turn off - SCREEN_DIM_WAKE_LOCK - Keep screen on but allow dimming - SCREEN_BRIGHT_WAKE_LOCK - Keep screen fully bright - FULL_WAKE_LOCK - Keep both CPU and screen running

docs.title: Alarm (Scheduled Tasks)
docs.text:
Android system timer service that allows apps to execute tasks at specific times or intervals.

Types: - RTC - Real-time clock based timer - RTC_WAKEUP - Real-time based timer that wakes device - ELAPSED_REALTIME - Timer based on device uptime - ELAPSED_REALTIME_WAKEUP - Uptime based timer that wakes device

docs.title: Service (Services)
docs.text:
Android app components that run in background without providing user interface.

Types: - Foreground Service - Performs user-noticeable tasks, shows persistent notification - Background Service - Performs tasks not directly noticeable to user - Bound Service - Provides client-server interface

docs.title: Doze Mode
docs.text: Power-saving mechanism introduced in Android 6.0+, device enters deep sleep when stationary.

docs.title: App Standby
docs.text: System power-saving restrictions on apps that haven't been used for extended periods.

docs.title: Background Execution Limits
docs.text: Android 8.0+ restrictions on background services and broadcast receivers.

docs.title: SELinux (Security-Enhanced Linux)
docs.text: Mandatory access control security mechanism in Android system.

docs.title: Xposed Framework
docs.text: Framework that allows modifying system and app behavior without modifying APKs.

docs.title: Hook
docs.text: Technique for intercepting and modifying function calls.

docs.title: LSPosed
docs.text: Modern Xposed implementation based on Riru, supports Android 8.1+.

docs.title: EdXposed
docs.text: Xposed implementation based on YAHFA and SandHook.

docs.title: Zygote
docs.text: Parent process of all app processes in Android system.

docs.title: Interception Modes
docs.text:
• Allow - No restrictions, normal operation
  • Restrict - Set time or frequency limits
  • Intercept - Completely block operation

docs.title: Rule System
docs.text: Configuration mechanism based on pattern matching, supports regular expressions.

docs.title: Components
docs.text: Collective term for WakeLocks, Alarms, and Services.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: Application range where Xposed module takes effect.

docs.title: DA
docs.text: Abbreviation for Detection/Action, refers to WakeLock, Alarm, Service activities detected by NoWakeLock.

docs.title: Acquisition Count
docs.text: Total number of times WakeLock was acquired.

docs.title: Cumulative Duration
docs.text: Total time WakeLock was held.

docs.title: Trigger Frequency
docs.text: Average trigger interval of Alarms.

docs.title: Start Count
docs.text: Total number of times Service was started.

docs.title: Interception Rate
docs.text: Percentage of intercepted operations out of total operations.

docs.title: API Level

Term Updates

As the project develops, new terms will be continuously added to this list. If you have questions, please refer to relevant documentation or contact the community.

Learning Suggestions

Recommend new users first familiarize themselves with core concepts (WakeLock, Alarm, Service), then gradually understand technical details.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Systematic problem diagnosis and solution guide.

docs.title: Module Status Verification
docs.text:
1. Open NoWakeLock → "Module Check"
  2. Confirm all items show ✅
  3. If any ❌ items, handle according to prompts

[Screenshot needed: Module check failure example]

docs.title: Xposed Framework Check
docs.text:
# Check LSPosed status
adb shell am start -n org.lsposed.manager/.ui.activity.MainActivity

# View module list
adb shell pm list packages | grep nowakelock

docs.title: Basic Permission Check
docs.text:
• Storage permission
  • Query all packages permission (Android 11+)
  • Accessibility service permission (if needed)

docs.title: WakeLock Testing
docs.text:
1. Set a simple WakeLock restriction rule
  2. Open corresponding app to trigger WakeLock
  3. Check statistics page for interception records

docs.title: Rule Effectiveness Testing
docs.text:
1. Create test rule: intercept specific WakeLock
  2. Observe target app behavior changes
  3. Check event logs to confirm rule execution

docs.title: Module Cannot Load
docs.text:
Symptoms: Module check shows "Module not loaded"

Diagnostic Steps: 1. Confirm Xposed framework running normally 2. Check if module is checked in manager 3. Verify app signature is correct

Solutions: 

# Reinstall module
adb uninstall com.js.nowakelock
adb install nowakelock.apk

# Clear framework cache
# In LSPosed: "Settings" → "Clear Cache"

docs.title: Hook Function Failed
docs.text:
Symptoms: Module loads but Hook doesn't work

Possible Causes: - System version incompatible - Scope configuration error - SELinux policy restrictions

Solutions: 1. Confirm scope includes android 2. Check SELinux status: 

adb shell getenforce
# If Enforcing, may affect Hook functionality
3. View Xposed logs: 
adb logcat | grep -E "(Xposed|nowakelock)"

docs.title: Rules Not Taking Effect
docs.text:
Symptoms: No interception effect after setting rules

Checklist: - [ ] Rules enabled - [ ] Match conditions correct - [ ] Target app restarted - [ ] No conflicting rules

Debug Methods: 1. Use simple exact matching for testing 2. Check rule priority 3. View matching logs

docs.title: App Function Abnormal
docs.text:
Symptoms: App cannot work normally after setting rules

Immediate Action: 1. Disable related rules 2. Restart problem app 3. Gradually restore rules

Root Solution: 1. Analyze critical components app depends on 2. Adjust rule scope or parameters 3. Use "Restrict" instead of "Intercept"

docs.title: Statistics Data Abnormal
docs.text:
Symptoms: Statistics data shows abnormally or doesn't update

Check Items: 1. Database status 

adb shell ls -la /data/data/com.js.nowakelock/databases/
2. Storage space 
adb shell df /data
3. App permissions

Fix Methods: 

# Clear database (Note: will lose historical data)
adb shell pm clear com.js.nowakelock

docs.title: System Lag
docs.text:
Symptoms: System response slows after installing NoWakeLock

Performance Analysis: 

# CPU usage
adb shell top | grep nowakelock

# Memory usage
adb shell dumpsys meminfo com.js.nowakelock

Optimization Solutions: 1. Reduce number of rules 2. Simplify regular expressions 3. Adjust statistics frequency

docs.title: Increased Battery Consumption
docs.text:
Symptoms: Module itself consumes battery

Diagnostic Methods: 1. Check background activity 

adb shell dumpsys battery
2. Analyze WakeLock usage 
adb shell dumpsys power | grep nowakelock

Solutions: - Check for abnormal loop tasks - Optimize database query frequency - Confirm no memory leaks

docs.title: Specific App Conflicts
docs.text:
Symptoms: Some apps conflict with NoWakeLock

Identification Methods: 1. System log analysis 2. App crash reports 3. ANR (Application Not Responding) logs

Handling Strategy: 

Temporary solution:
  - Add app to whitelist
  - Disable related rules

Long-term solution:
  - Analyze conflict causes
  - Adjust Hook strategy
  - Update compatibility code

docs.title: System Version Compatibility
docs.text:
Symptoms: Function abnormal on new Android versions

Adaptation Check: 1. API change analysis 2. Permission model changes 3. Security policy updates

Downgrade Plan: - Disable incompatible features - Use alternative implementations - Wait for version updates

docs.title: System Logs
docs.text:
# Complete logs
adb logcat -v time > full_log.txt

# NoWakeLock related
adb logcat | grep -i nowakelock > nowakelock_log.txt

# Xposed related
adb logcat | grep -i xposed > xposed_log.txt

docs.title: App Logs
docs.text:
# Specific process logs
adb logcat --pid=$(adb shell pidof com.js.nowakelock)

# Crash logs
adb logcat | grep -E "(FATAL|AndroidRuntime)"

docs.title: Key Error Identifiers
docs.text:
E/Xposed: Hook failed
E/NoWakeLock: Database error
W/ActivityManager: Unable to start service

docs.title: Performance Issue Identifiers
docs.text:
W/Choreographer: Skipped frames
I/Timeline: Timeline: Activity_idle
W/InputDispatcher: Application is not responding

docs.title: Log Cleanup
docs.text:
# Clear logs
adb logcat -c

# Set log level
adb shell setprop log.tag.NoWakeLock VERBOSE

docs.title: Configuration Backup
docs.text:
# Backup configuration
adb backup -f backup.ab com.js.nowakelock

# Extract database
adb shell cp /data/data/com.js.nowakelock/databases/app_database /sdcard/
adb pull /sdcard/app_database ./

docs.title: Configuration Restore
docs.text:
# Restore backup
adb restore backup.ab

# Manual database restore
adb push ./app_database /sdcard/
adb shell cp /sdcard/app_database /data/data/com.js.nowakelock/databases/

docs.title: Soft Reset (Keep Configuration)
docs.text:
1. App settings → Clear cache
  2. Restart app

docs.title: Hard Reset (Clear All Data)
docs.text: adb shell pm clear com.js.nowakelock

docs.title: Complete Reset (Reinstall)
docs.text:
adb uninstall com.js.nowakelock
# Reinstall and configure
